methods.description: Analysis of expression patterns of 125 co-expressed differentially expressed genes (DEGs) in CK-VS-LT and CK-VS-NO libraries using cluster analysis to classify genes into groups based on similar expression patterns.

steps.description: Perform a cluster analysis of the gene expression patterns between CK-VS-LT and CK-VS-NO libraries for the 125 co-expressed DEGs.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis on gene expression data
inputs: List of 125 co-expressed DEGs (Additional file 8: Table S7)
outputs: Clustering results of gene expression patterns

steps.description: Classify these genes into three groups based on the cluster analysis results.
steps.searchable_summary: classify genes into groups based on clustering
outputs: Grouped list of 125 co-expressed DEGs (e.g., Group 1, Group 3)

methods.method_name: De Novo Transcriptome Assembly and Data Filtering
methods.description: Processing raw sequencing reads to remove low-quality data and adapters, assembling clean reads into unigenes and homologous transcript clusters, and annotating the assembled sequences.

steps.description: Remove dirty reads containing unknown or low-quality bases and adapters from raw sequencing reads.
steps.searchable_summary: filter raw sequencing reads for quality and adapters
outputs: Reads with adapters and low-quality bases removed

steps.description: Remove reads with more than 5 % unknown nucleotides.
steps.searchable_summary: filter sequencing reads based on unknown nucleotide percentage

steps.description: Remove low-quality reads (>20 % read rate with a quality value ≤ 10).
steps.searchable_summary: filter sequencing reads based on quality score

steps.description: Use Trinity software to assemble clean reads into unigenes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform de novo transcriptome assembly using Trinity

steps.description: Use TGICL software to further account for splicing and redundancy, assembling unigenes into homologous transcript clusters.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster unigenes and remove redundancy using TGICL
outputs: Homologous transcript clusters (CL sequences) and singletons (unigenes)

steps.description: Apply the same process (steps 1-5) to all three samples to obtain the longest possible non-redundant unigenes.
steps.searchable_summary: process multiple samples and obtain non-redundant unigenes
inputs: Raw sequencing reads from all three samples
outputs: Longest possible non-redundant unigenes across all samples

steps.description: Align unigenes by BLASTX (E-value < 0.00001) to protein databases (in order of priority) NR, Swiss-Prot, KEGG and COG.
steps.searchable_summary: align unigenes to protein databases using BLASTX

steps.description: Use the proteins showing the highest ranks in the results of BLAST search to determine the coding region sequences of the unigenes.
steps.searchable_summary: determine coding region sequences based on best BLAST hits
outputs: Predicted coding region sequences (CDS) for unigenes

steps.description: Use ESTS software to determine the sequence direction, if a unigene could not align with any of the above databases.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sequence direction using ESTS for unannotated unigenes
inputs: Unigenes that did not align to protein databases in step 7
outputs: Sequence direction (5' to 3') for unannotated unigenes

steps.description: Produce the nucleotide sequence in the direction from 5' to 3' and the amino sequence of the predicted coding region.
steps.searchable_summary: generate nucleotide and amino acid sequences from predicted CDS
inputs: Predicted coding region sequences from step 8
outputs: Amino acid sequences of predicted proteins

steps.description: Deposit all sequencing data at the sequence read archive of NCBI (Accession number SRR3270364, SRR3270376, SRR3270829, SRR3270928, SRR3270974, SRR3270993, SRR3270997, SRR3271001 and SRR3271002).
steps.searchable_summary: deposit sequencing data to NCBI SRA
inputs: Raw sequencing data from all samples
